title: medicago littoralis subsp. striata
intro: Medicago littoralis subsp. striata, also known as striate medick or striate medica, is a plant species belonging to the legume family and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Medicago littoralis subsp. striata include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and protect against cardiovascular disease. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and has also been used in food and feed applications due to its nutritional value.
intro_study: According to "Medycyna nowozytna : studia nad historia medycyny", Medicago littoralis subsp. striata benefits were likely utilized in traditional medical practices for the recovery and protection against diseases, including treatment for wounds, ulcers, and fever, although specific information about this subspecies is not provided in the study.
